story.text: A vocabulary rename (organ->part, metabolism->cycle) kept RECURRING as stranded one-off sed branches that each re-decided the same swap by hand and broke grammar on the way ('an part that senses', misaligned tables). The recurrence WAS the per-file re-decision. This recasts a rename as the repo's own spine applied to vocabulary: the decision is an ADMITTED RECORD on the log (loop.rename admit, written once, signed --by, superseding like a tag - never a constant in code), and application is a DETERMINISTIC, idempotent fold over the live tree (loop.rename apply). Re-running status reports zero in-scope occurrences - the checkable form of 'it stops occurring'. A handle-safe boundary preserves identifiers/filenames/handles; the existing phrasing checker gates each edit so the prose tier is provably prose-only; census.py's wholesale identifier rename is the code tier. It also repairs the 'an part' article damage prior sed left on main.

briefing.headline: rename as a recorded decision + a deterministic applier (loop/rename.py) - so a rename stops recurring as stranded sed branches
briefing.value: loop/rename.py: `admit --from X --to Y --by bdo` writes the rename decision ONCE as a `rename` admission (folded by admitted_renames, latest-per-source wins, withdraw drops - the tags.py shape). `apply` is a deterministic, idempotent fold over the in-scope tree with a handle-safe word boundary (joins on `_ . / -`, plus a (?!\.\w) guard so a sentence period renames but `.md` does not) and an article-repair pass that heals a prior rename's 'an part' wherever it sits. Each edit is gated by loop.phrasing.phrasing_only (I-4), so the prose tier is provably prose-only and lands via the light lane; a file whose rename would touch a code token (census.py identifiers) is refused and flagged for the code tier.
briefing.why_now: organ->part / metabolism->cycle was landing piecemeal and breaking grammar; bdo (2026-06-22) named the fix: 'we write down that it was renamed, ONCE, then we rename it EVERYWHERE.' Without a recorded decision + deterministic applier, every future rename is another hand-sed and another stranded branch.
briefing.if_accepted: A rename is decided once and applied everywhere, reproducibly; re-running status proves zero in-scope occurrences remain. organ->part and metabolism->cycle are fully applied (prose + code tiers), the prior 'an part' damage repaired, and any future rename is one admit line + a re-run.
briefing.if_rejected: Renames keep recurring as one-off sed branches that re-decide the swap and strand work, with broken grammar accreting on main.
briefing.cost_of_wrong_call: Low and bounded. loop/rename.py is stdlib, no network, no git; it composes the existing phrasing checker rather than a second checker. Reverting is deleting the pen + test and superseding the two rename admissions (history stands). The teeth are proven non-vacuous: tests/test_rename.py renames bare words + repairs articles, refuses code tokens via the phrasing gate, preserves compounds/handles, and excludes the record trees.
briefing.mechanism: loop/rename.py: admitted_renames folds `rename` admissions (latest-per-source, withdraw drops); rename_text applies each rule with the handle-safe boundary + article repair (pure, idempotent); _in_scope excludes log/done/reports/atoms/epics/agendas/nodes, docs/phase-2, docs/sources, exports, generated outputs, and the self-referential pen/test; apply re-proves phrasing_only at the write seam and records one rename_applied admission. The two decisions are on the log (organ->part, metabolism->cycle, by bdo). tests/test_rename.py is the §10 spec.
